step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the previous agent. Packed Red Blood Cell (PRBC) transfusions indeed raise Hematocrit (HCT) levels. While there can be variability based on individual patient factors such as baseline volume status and underlying disease conditions, studies generally show an approximate rise of 3-5% per unit of PRBC administered. This aligns well with the consensus literature and practical experience in clinical settings.

Therefore, my final answer remains: **C**. One unit of PRBC raises HCT by approximately 3-5%. Your agreement acknowledges both the accuracy of the provided information and its relevance within typical clinical scenarios where this knowledge would be applied effectively. 

Final answer: **C**.
